utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: patrick allen . date of birth : 7/7/1977 . new patient visit . past medical history includes gerd , anxiety , depression . here for chronic abdominal pain . he had an abdominal ct on 1/23/2020 . impression is a normal ct of the ab- abdomen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hello , are you mr. allen ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi . my name is dr. edwards . nice to meet you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice to meet you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: welcome to the gi specialty clinic .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: did you have any problems finding us ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i've been here with my sister once before .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . so how can i help you today ? uh , the referral i have is for abdominal pain and diarrhea .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right . so i've had ... i've been having this pain right here in my stomach , like right around here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so in the area of your mid abdomen , just below the belly button ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: correct . i've had the pain on and off for about two years . i finally went to the er and a ... a few months ago and they did a ct scan .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i saw that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . they said they did n't really see anything on the scan .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes , i agree . it looked normal .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: the problem is i'm either constipated or have explosive diarrhea .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is the pain there all the time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's a nagging feeling and it just depends . sometimes it bothers me , sometimes it does n't .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: has this been the case over the past two years as well ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: more recently in the past couple months , at least with the constipation and diarrhea .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and before that , how are your bowel movements ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they were normal .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , okay . so any blood in your stool ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you feel like you have more constipation or diarrhea ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: probably more constipation .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so when you're constipated , do you not have a bowel movement or is the stool hard ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i usually do n't go , but when i do , it's hard .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how often do you have a bowel movement when you are constipated ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: about three to four times a week . it's like when i need to go to the bathroom , if i can massage it , it feels like it's moving some and i can eventually go .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and when you have a bowel movement , does the pain change ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , it gets a little better .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and are you eating and drinking okay ? any nausea or vomiting , heartburn or indigestion ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: none of that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so tell me about the diarrhea , how often do you get it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it kinda just depends on what i eat . i think i have a very sensitive stomach . if i eat pasta with a creamy sauce , i'm probably gon na have diarrhea .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and it does n't happen for multiple days in a row or is it just one time ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's usually just one time and then it's over .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how's your weight been ? any fluctuation ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nice and pretty stable , although i could stand to lose about 25 pounds .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and is there any family history of gi issues that you know of ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not that i can think of . well , actually my sister does have problems with her stomach too . she has irritable bowel syndrome and that is kind of what i always thought i had even thought i've never been diagnosed with it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and is there any family history of gi cancer or liver disease ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: have you ever had any surgeries on your abdomen ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've never had any surgery .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so your gallbladder , appendix , all those are still intact ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yup .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and have you ever had a colonoscopy ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . i thought that happen when you turn 50 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , that's for colon cancer screening , but there are other reasons to have a colonoscopy , like unexplained abdominal pain and changes in bowel habits .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , come have a seat here and lay back so i can examine you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na start by listening to your belly with my steth- stethoscope . and i hear bowel sounds in all four quadrants .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: what does that mean ? is everything okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it just means that i can hear little noises in all areas of your belly , which means your bowels are active and working .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so now , i'm going to push on your upper and lower abdomen . let me know if you have any pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it hurts a little when you push right there on the left side , near my belly button .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i do feel stool in your lower colon , which would coincide with constipation , but i also feel a slight enlargement of your liver here on the upper right side . have you had any lab work done recently ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i have a physical about four months ago and they ... i had blood drawn then .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and did your primary care physician say anything about the lab results ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: he said i had some very slightly elevated liver enzymes , but we would recheck them in about six months .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you remember what enzymes were elevated , alt , ast , alp ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: he said the alt and the ast were elevated .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and do you take any medications , either prescription or over-the-counter ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i take crestor and olmesartan daily and then tylenol for occasion- occasional pain .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how frequently do you take the tylenol ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hardly ever . maybe once a month .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and do you consume alcohol ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , yes , but only a couple of beers after working in the yard on saturdays .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and no previous history of heavy alcohol or drug use ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and have you had any recent issues with excessive bruising or bleeding ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how about any issues with your ankles or feet swelling ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i'm gon na take a look at your eyes and skin . i do n't see any jaundice .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: what would cause that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: issues with your liver . let me take a quick listen to your heart and lungs .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: lungs are clear , bilateral heart sounds are normal , no murmurs , gallops , or rubs noted .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yes . the rest of your physical exam is normal other than what seems to be an increased stool burden in your colon and a slight hepatomegaly .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: what's that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: increase stool burden means that there's a lot of stool sitting in your colon .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and that's the constipation , right ? but what about the other thing ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: the hepatomegaly means the liver is enlarged .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but you said mine was slightly enlarged ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: correct .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so what does that mean ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , let's talk about what we found and then some possible next steps if you're in agreement .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so as i said , the hepatomegaly means your liver is enlarged .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: could that be why my stomach is hurting and i'm having issues with the constipation and diarrhea ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no , i think you're constipated and have occasional bouts of diarrhea because of certain foods you eat . and we can get you started right away on a fiber supplement that should help with that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so what about my liver ? why is it enlarged ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , there are many reasons why people can have an elevated liver enzymes and also enlarged liver . some possible causes are certain medications that can be toxic to liver , alcohol abuse , fatty liver disease , hepatitis , cirrhosis , and other liver diseases like wilson's disease .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so what do i need to do ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well , i think since it's been about four months since your blood work was done , we should check your liver enzymes in addition to a few other labs .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . and then what ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: we will get those drawn today and then depending upon the results you may need an ultrasound of your liver . i think we need to talk about your medications too .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: which medications ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: crestor , how long have you been taking that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: about 18 months .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . well , crestor is one of the medications that can cause liver toxicity so it may be a good idea to discuss other alternatives .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: should i talk to my primary care or can you change it ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i would recommend calling your primary care and discuss that with him since he follows you for your blood pressure and cholesterol .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . i'll call him this afternoon .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . i also think we should go ahead and get you scheduled for a liver ultrasound . if your blood work looks good , then we can always cancel that .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . when do you think i'll be able to get the ultrasound done ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hopefully , within the next two weeks . you will receive a call from the radiology scheduling this afternoon to get it set up .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . and then what happens ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: when i get the results from the test , i will contact you . and depending upon what we find , we'll come up with our next steps .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and when should i see you again ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , let's schedule an appointment when you check out to return in four weeks . we'll discuss how you're doing with the fiber supplement and your constipation and review test results to determine if we need to do further testing on your liver .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . is there anything else i can do to help with these issues ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: definitely refrain from drinking any alcohol , increase your water intake to at least 48 ounces a day in addition to taking the fiber supplement to help with your constipation . and be mindful of eating foods that you were sensitive to so you can avoid the bouts of diarrhea .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . and i'll talk to my primary care about my crestor .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: excellent . and do you have any other questions for me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't think so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . so remember when you check out the front desk , schedule follow-up appointment with me for four weeks and then go to the lab to get your blood work drawn .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and expect a call from radiology scheduling about setting up your ultrasound .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right . thanks , dr. edwards .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: thank you , mr. allen .

detail_list.summary: Patrick Allen is a 42-year-old male with a history of GERD, anxiety, and depression presenting for a new patient visit.
The patient had a normal abdominal CT scan on 01/23/2020.
Abdominal pain and diarrhea.
The patient reports intermittent mid-abdominal pain localized inferior to the umbilicus for approximately 2 years.
The patient reports symptoms of constipation and episodic diarrhea for the past 2 months.
The doctor asks if the abdominal pain is constant.
The patient describes the pain as a nagging feeling that fluctuates in intensity.
The doctor asks if there is any blood in the patient's stool.
The patient denies blood in his stool.
The doctor asks whether the patient experiences more constipation or diarrhea.
The patient reports experiencing more constipation than diarrhea.
The doctor asks about the frequency of bowel movements during constipation.
The patient has 3-4 bowel movements per week when constipated and finds abdominal massage helpful.
The doctor asks if the pain changes after a bowel movement.
The patient reports that the pain improves slightly after a bowel movement.
The doctor asks about nausea, vomiting, heartburn, indigestion, and dietary intake.
The patient denies nausea, vomiting, heartburn, and indigestion, and confirms normal eating and drinking.
The patient notes that pasta with creamy sauce triggers episodes of diarrhea.
The doctor asks about weight fluctuations.
The patient reports stable weight but expresses a desire to lose 25 pounds.
The doctor asks about family history of gastrointestinal issues.
The patient's sister has irritable bowel syndrome.
The doctor asks about family history of gastrointestinal cancer or liver disease.
The patient denies family history of gastrointestinal cancer or liver disease.
The doctor asks about prior abdominal surgeries.
The patient denies any prior abdominal surgeries.
The doctor asks if the patient has ever had a colonoscopy.
The patient has never had a colonoscopy.
Physical exam reveals normoactive bowel sounds in all four quadrants.
Physical exam reveals mild left periumbilical tenderness.
Physical exam reveals increased stool burden in the colon and mild hepatomegaly.
The patient reports mildly elevated liver enzymes (AST and ALT) from bloodwork 4 months ago.
The patient takes Crestor and olmesartan daily, and Tylenol as needed.
The doctor asks about alcohol consumption.
The patient drinks 2 beers once a week.
The doctor asks about excessive bruising or bleeding.
The patient denies excessive bruising or bleeding.
The doctor asks about lower extremity edema.
The patient denies lower extremity edema.
Physical exam shows no jaundice.
Physical exam shows clear lungs and normal heart sounds without murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
The doctor recommends starting a daily fiber supplement for constipation.
The doctor orders repeat liver enzyme labs.
The doctor plans for a potential liver ultrasound depending on lab results.
The patient has been taking Crestor for 18 months.
The doctor advises the patient to contact his primary care provider to discuss alternative medications for Crestor.
The doctor schedules a liver ultrasound.
The patient is to return to the clinic in 4 weeks.
The doctor instructs the patient to cease alcohol consumption, increase water intake to 48 ounces daily, and avoid trigger foods.
